questions.stem:
—______ you ever ______ to the new science museum?
—Yes, I ______ there last Sunday with my classmates.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的辨析。问句中'ever'（曾经）是现在完成时的标志词，且表示'去过某地（已回）'用have been to；答句中'last Sunday'是明确的过去时间状语，需用一般过去时went。A、D项第一空错误，C项第二空have gone表示'去了未回'与语境不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my best friend, Bosco.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'the boy'指人，且在从句中作主语，应用who。which指物；whose表示所属关系；whom指人但作宾语。口诀：先行词是人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who/that，所属用whose；指物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ______ to the school library yesterday.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。主语'books'与动词'return'是被动关系（书被归还），时间状语'yesterday'表明用一般过去时，结构为was/were+过去分词。主语是复数，用were returned。A是主动原形；B是主动过去式；D是一般现在时被动，时态不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you think ______ we will have a picnic tomorrow?
—I'm not sure. It ______ rain in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句连接词和情态动词表推测。第一空：think后接宾语从句且不缺成分时用that（口语中可省），if/whether虽然也可用，但第二空是关键。根据答句'I'm not sure'（不确定），第二空需用might（可能）表不确定推测；must表肯定推测（一定），与'not sure'矛盾。因此选B。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词词义辨析及固定搭配。be satisfied with是固定搭配，意为'对……满意'。句意：如果你满足于目前的分数，你应该更努力取得更好的成绩。exciting（令人兴奋的）修饰物；interested常与in搭配；surprised常与at/by搭配。根据句意和搭配，选C。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you ______ the prefix 'un' from 'unhappy', you get the word 'happy'.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义及构词法。prefix意为'前缀'，从'unhappy'中去掉'un'得到'happy'，应用remove（移除）。add（添加）意思相反；build（建立）和create（创造）与构词法操作无关。注意：un-是常见否定前缀，如unfair, unable等。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The government is trying to ______ the environment by planting more trees.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义辨析。improve（改善、提高）可与environment搭配，表示'改善环境'。increase（增加）常指数量的增长；rise（上升）是不及物动词；grow（种植、生长）虽然与trees相关，但'grow the environment'搭配不当。根据宾语'environment'，只能选improve。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理。上文提到塑料垃圾进入海洋，下文说'hurts the whole ecosystem'（伤害整个生态系统），此处应选一个相对概括的词。affects（影响）涵盖范围最广，且与hurt形成递进关系。kills（杀死）过于绝对；pollutes（污染）与前文'pollution'重复；endangers（危及）虽可接受，但affects更中性且符合说明文语气。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a meaningful activity called "Green Campus Week". The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting our environment.

On Monday morning, our teacher gave us a speech about plastic pollution. She told us that about 8 million tons of plastic waste enters the ocean every year. This (8)______ many sea animals and hurts the whole ecosystem. After listening to the speech, we all felt that we should take action immediately.

During the week, each class was asked to (9)______ a poster about environmental protection. My classmate Bosco and I worked together. We (10)______ pictures of polluted rivers and clean forests on the poster. We also wrote a slogan: "Save the Earth, Save Our Future". Our poster won the first prize, (11)______ made us very proud.

Besides making posters, we also started a recycling program. We collected used bottles and paper in the classroom. By the end of the week, we had collected over 200 bottles. The money from selling them would be (12)______ to buy books for children in poor areas.

This activity taught us that even small actions can make a big difference to our planet.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词与名词搭配。create a poster（制作海报）是固定搭配。buy（买）和borrow（借）与语境不符，海报是学生自己做的；lend（借出）也不合适。根据下文描述的画图和写标语，可知是'创作/制作'海报。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a meaningful activity called "Green Campus Week". The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting our environment.

On Monday morning, our teacher gave us a speech about plastic pollution. She told us that about 8 million tons of plastic waste enters the ocean every year. This (8)______ many sea animals and hurts the whole ecosystem. After listening to the speech, we all felt that we should take action immediately.

During the week, each class was asked to (9)______ a poster about environmental protection. My classmate Bosco and I worked together. We (10)______ pictures of polluted rivers and clean forests on the poster. We also wrote a slogan: "Save the Earth, Save Our Future". Our poster won the first prize, (11)______ made us very proud.

Besides making posters, we also started a recycling program. We collected used bottles and paper in the classroom. By the end of the week, we had collected over 200 bottles. The money from selling them would be (12)______ to buy books for children in poor areas.

This activity taught us that even small actions can make a big difference to our planet.

questions.explanation: 本题考查语境线索。根据'on the poster'（在海报上）和'pictures'（图画），可知是在海报上'drew'（画）了河流和森林的图片。took（拍照）、cut（剪）、found（发现）均不符合'制作海报'的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a meaningful activity called "Green Campus Week". The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting our environment.

On Monday morning, our teacher gave us a speech about plastic pollution. She told us that about 8 million tons of plastic waste enters the ocean every year. This (8)______ many sea animals and hurts the whole ecosystem. After listening to the speech, we all felt that we should take action immediately.

During the week, each class was asked to (9)______ a poster about environmental protection. My classmate Bosco and I worked together. We (10)______ pictures of polluted rivers and clean forests on the poster. We also wrote a slogan: "Save the Earth, Save Our Future". Our poster won the first prize, (11)______ made us very proud.

Besides making posters, we also started a recycling program. We collected used bottles and paper in the classroom. By the end of the week, we had collected over 200 bottles. The money from selling them would be (12)______ to buy books for children in poor areas.

This activity taught us that even small actions can make a big difference to our planet.

questions.explanation: 本题考查非限制性定语从句。逗号后指代前面整句话'Our poster won the first prize'，且作主语，应用which引导非限制性定语从句。who指人；what不能引导定语从句；it会造成逗号连接两个独立句子的语法错误（逗号粘连）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a meaningful activity called "Green Campus Week". The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting our environment.

On Monday morning, our teacher gave us a speech about plastic pollution. She told us that about 8 million tons of plastic waste enters the ocean every year. This (8)______ many sea animals and hurts the whole ecosystem. After listening to the speech, we all felt that we should take action immediately.

This activity taught us that even small actions can make a big difference to our planet.

questions.explanation: 本题考查被动语态及固定搭配。be used to do sth（被用来做某事）。句意：卖瓶子的钱将被用来给贫困地区的孩子买书。spent主语通常是人；taken（拿走）不符合语境；paid（支付）常与for搭配。根据句意选used。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: How many hours of sleep do teenagers need every night according to Dr. Wang?
questions.explanation: 本题考查细节理解。根据文章第二段第一句'teenagers need at least 8-9 hours of sleep every night'可知，青少年每晚需要8-9小时睡眠。直接定位原文即可得出答案。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Many students in Grade 8 often stay up late to finish their homework. They think sleeping less can give them more time to study. However, Dr. Wang, a sleep scientist, says this idea is completely wrong.

According to Dr. Wang's research, teenagers need at least 8-9 hours of sleep every night. During sleep, our brain works like a computer cleaning up its memory. It stores important information and removes unnecessary things. If you don't get enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well. As a result, you will feel tired and find it hard to concentrate in class.

What's more, lack of sleep affects your mood too. Students who sleep less than 6 hours often feel anxious and stressed. They may get angry easily over small things. "Sleep is not a waste of time," Dr. Wang explains. "It is actually an important part of learning."

To sleep better, Dr. Wang suggests three things. First, turn off your mobile phone an hour before bedtime. The blue light from screens makes it hard for you to fall asleep. Second, don't drink coffee or tea in the evening. Third, try to go to bed at the same time every night. This helps your body build a healthy rhythm.

Remember, good sleep habits don't just help you feel better. They can also improve your grades. So tonight, try to finish your homework earlier and give your brain the rest it needs.

questions.stem: What does the word "rhythm" in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
options: A. A regular pattern of actions
B. A kind of physical exercise
C. A way of eating food
D. A type of sleep problem
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'go to bed at the same time every night'（每晚同一时间睡觉）和'helps your body build...'，可知这是帮助身体建立规律的'节奏/节律'。regular pattern of actions（有规律的行为模式）符合语境。其他选项与'固定时间睡觉'无关。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:

According to Dr. Wang's research, teenagers need at least 8-9 hours of sleep every night. During sleep, our brain works like a computer cleaning up its memory. It stores important information and removes unnecessary things. If you don't get enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well. As a result, you will feel tired and find it hard to concentrate in class.

What's more, lack of sleep affects your mood too. Students who sleep less than 6 hours often feel anxious and stressed. They may get angry easily over small things. "Sleep is not a waste of time," Dr. Wang explains. "It is actually an important part of learning."

To sleep better, Dr. Wang suggests three things. First, turn off your mobile phone an hour before bedtime. The blue light from screens makes it hard for you to fall asleep. Second, don't drink coffee or tea in the evening. Third, try to go to bed at the same time every night. This helps your body build a healthy rhythm.

Remember, good sleep habits don't just help you feel better. They can also improve your grades. So tonight, try to finish your homework earlier and give your brain the rest it needs.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Wang's words in the passage?
options: A. Students should do homework as late as possible
B. Sleeping well can help students study better
C. Mobile phones help teenagers fall asleep quickly
D. Drinking coffee before bed is good for sleep
questions.explanation: 本题考查推理判断。根据最后一段'good sleep habits...can also improve your grades'（良好的睡眠习惯能提高成绩）可推断出，睡得好能帮助学生学得更好。A与文意相反；C、D与第四段建议'turn off mobile phone'和'don't drink coffee'相悖。做推理题需基于原文，排除与原文矛盾或没有依据的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Many students in Grade 8 often stay up late to finish their homework. They think sleeping less can give them more time to study. However, Dr. Wang, a sleep scientist, says this idea is completely wrong.

According to Dr. Wang's research, teenagers need at least 8-9 hours of sleep every night. During sleep, our brain works like a computer cleaning up its memory. It stores important information and removes unnecessary things. If you don't get enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well. To sleep better, Dr. Wang suggests three things. First, turn off your mobile phone an hour before bedtime. The blue light from screens makes it hard for you to fall asleep. Second, don't drink coffee or tea in the evening. Third, try to go to bed at the same time every night. This helps your body build a healthy rhythm.

Remember, good sleep habits don't just help you feel better. They can also improve your grades. So tonight, try to finish your homework earlier and give your brain the rest it needs.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Finish Homework Quickly
B. The Bad Effects of Mobile Phones
C. Why Sleep Matters for Students
D. Ways to Become a Sleep Scientist
questions.explanation: 本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章开篇批驳'少睡觉多学习'的错误观念，中间阐述睡眠对记忆和情绪的重要性，最后给出建议。全文围绕'睡眠对学生的重要性'展开。A、B、D都是文章局部细节，不能概括全文。选标题要抓住高频词（sleep）和作者核心观点。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Many students in Grade 8 often stay up late to finish their homework. They think sleeping less can give them more time to study. However, Dr. Wang, a sleep scientist, says this idea is completely wrong.

According to Dr. Wang's research, teenagers need at least 8-9 hours of sleep every night. During sleep, our brain works like a computer cleaning up its memory. It stores important information and removes unnecessary things. If you don't get enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well. As a result, you will feel tired and find it hard to concentrate in class.

What's more, lack of sleep affects your mood too. Students who sleep less than 6 hours often feel anxious and stressed. They may get angry easily over small things. "Sleep is not a waste of time," Dr. Wang explains. "It is actually an important part of learning."

To sleep better, Dr. Wang suggests three things. First, turn off your mobile phone an hour before bedtime. The blue light from screens makes it hard for you to fall asleep. Second, don't drink coffee or tea in the evening. Third, try to go to bed at the same time every night. This helps your body build a healthy rhythm.

Remember, good sleep habits don't just help you feel better. They can also improve your grades. So tonight, try to finish your homework earlier and give your brain the rest it needs.

options: A. The first step is to make a weekly plan.
B. However, you should adjust your hobby time according to your study schedule.
C. In addition, try to combine short hobby breaks with study time.
D. Finally, remember that balance is the key to a healthy school life.
E. On the other hand, never spend time on hobbies during school days.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落主旨句。空格后提到'First, write down all the things...'（首先写下所有事情），说明本段讲的是制定计划。A项'第一步是制定周计划'作为段首主题句最合适。E项过于绝对且与文章主旨'平衡'相悖，是干扰项。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students have trouble balancing their schoolwork and hobbies. They often ask, "How can I do well in studies and still have time for my interests?" Here are some useful tips.

(17)______. First, write down all the things you need to do, including homework and hobby time. This helps you see clearly how much time you actually have. You can use a planner or an app on your phone.

(18)______. If you love playing basketball, don't do it for three hours every day during exam weeks. Instead, practice for just 30 minutes. You can play longer during weekends or holidays.

(19)______. Use short breaks between study sessions for your hobbies. For example, if you enjoy drawing, sketch something quickly during your 10-minute break. This refreshes your mind and makes you more focused when you return to studying.

(20)______. Remember that your studies are important, but hobbies help you relax and develop skills. Don't give up your interests completely, even when you are busy.

options: A. The first step is to make a weekly plan.
B. However, you should adjust your hobby time according to your study schedule.
C. In addition, try to combine short hobby breaks with study time.
D. Finally, remember that balance is the key to a healthy school life.
E. On the other hand, never spend time on hobbies during school days.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系（转折）。空格后提到'don't do it for three hours every day during exam weeks'（考试周不要玩三小时），说明要根据学习安排调整爱好时间。B项'然而，你应该根据学习安排调整爱好时间'引出下文，However表示转折，符合语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students have trouble balancing their schoolwork and hobbies. They often ask, "How can I do well in studies and still have time for my interests?" Here are some useful tips.

(17)______. First, write down all the things you need to do, including homework and hobby time. This helps you see clearly how much time you actually have. You can use a planner or an app on your phone.

(18)______. If you love playing basketball, don't do it for three hours every day during exam weeks. Instead, practice for just 30 minutes. You can play longer during weekends or holidays.

(19)______. Use short breaks between study sessions for your hobbies. For example, if you enjoy drawing, sketch something quickly during your 10-minute break. This refreshes your mind and makes you more focused when you return to studying.

(20)______. Remember that your studies are important, but hobbies help you relax and develop skills. Don't give up your interests completely, even when you are busy.

options: A. The first step is to make a weekly plan.
B. However, you should adjust your hobby time according to your study schedule.
C. In addition, try to combine short hobby breaks with study time.
D. Finally, remember that balance is the key to a healthy school life.
E. On the other hand, never spend time on hobbies during school days.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系（递进）。空格后提到'Use short breaks between study sessions for your hobbies'（利用学习间隙的短休息进行爱好活动），C项'此外，尝试将短暂的爱好休息与学习时间结合'正好概括此内容。In addition表示递进，引出第三条建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students have trouble balancing their schoolwork and hobbies. They often ask, "How can I do well in studies and still have time for my interests?" Here are some useful tips.

options: A. The first step is to make a weekly plan.
B. However, you should adjust your hobby time according to your study schedule.
C. In addition, try to combine short hobby breaks with study time.
D. Finally, remember that balance is the key to a healthy school life.
E. On the other hand, never spend time on hobbies during school days.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系（总结）。空格位于最后一段段首，后文总结'学习重要，但爱好也重要，不要完全放弃兴趣'，强调平衡的重要性。D项'最后，记住平衡是健康学校生活的关键'作为总结句，Finally表示顺序最后，也呼应文章结尾的总结功能。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students have trouble balancing their schoolwork and hobbies. They often ask, "How can I do well in studies and still have time for my interests?" Here are some useful tips.

(20)______. Remember that your studies are important, but hobbies help you relax and develop skills. Don't give up your interests completely, even when you are busy.
